event: Japanese mother (Yamanba) holding child (Kintaro) with a sake cup
description: A Japanese ukiyo-e woodblock print showing a woman with wild, voluminous black hair looking down tenderly at a small red-skinned child she holds in her arms. The child grasps a large flat sake cup or dish. The woman wears a patterned kimono and her expression is gentle and maternal. The child appears strong and robust with a round face. Japanese characters appear on the left side of the composition. The style is characteristic of Kitagawa Utamaro's elegant figure prints.
